text: Data Structures and Algorithms include arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, sorting algorithms, searching techniques, recursion, dynamic programming, and greedy methods.
Time and space complexity are used to evaluate algorithm performance. Big-O notation describes upper bounds.
Programming fundamentals include variables, loops, conditionals, functions, data types, memory models, and error handling.
Object-oriented programming concepts include classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction, and encapsulation.
Design principles like SOLID help build scalable object-oriented systems.
Operating Systems manage processes, threads, scheduling algorithms, deadlocks, semaphores, memory allocation, virtual memory, paging, and synchronization.
DBMS includes ACID properties, indexing, SQL queries, normalization forms, transactions, isolation levels, and concurrency control.
Indexes improve query performance through B-trees or hashing structures.
Computer Networks include TCP, UDP, IP routing, congestion control, HTTP, DNS, network layers, and packet transmission.
Backend development involves REST APIs, authentication, databases, caching, queuing systems, and server-side business logic.
Backend technologies include Node.js, Django, Flask, Express, and serverless architectures.
Frontend development involves HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, component-based UI, DOM manipulation, and responsive design.
SQL covers joins, aggregations, filtering, grouping, indexing, stored procedures, triggers, and relational schema design.
System design covers scalability, load balancing, caching, sharding, replication, consistency models, CAP theorem, and distributed systems.
Git is used for version control with commands like commit, push, pull, merge, and branching. CI/CD automates testing and deployments.
DevOps involves CI/CD, containerization, Docker, Kubernetes, infrastructure as code, monitoring, and automation pipelines.
Machine learning includes supervised and unsupervised learning, classification, regression, neural networks, training, evaluation metrics, and feature engineering.
Data analytics involves descriptive analysis, data visualization, feature extraction, hypothesis testing, and predictive modeling.
Business analytics focuses on business metrics, KPIs, forecasting, decision-making frameworks, and dashboard interpretation.
Product management involves user research, feature prioritization, roadmapping, metrics, experimentation, and cross-functional communication.
